reviews.comment: Everyone is nice and professional. I think they understand kids and don"t mind if parents come in the room. I go in with my child to keep things calm. I stay quiet unless we need further explanation on something.
reviews.response:
Thank you Patricia for taking the time to share your experience at our office. We're thrilled to hear that our staff was able to make you and your child feel comfortable and at ease during your visit.

At our facility, we strive to provide a welcoming and professional environment for both parents and children. We understand that some children may feel anxious or nervous during medical appointments, and we are dedicated to doing our best to ensure that each visit is as stress-free as possible.

We appreciate your feedback about our staff's ability to explain things clearly, and we always encourage parents to ask questions or seek further clarification if needed. We believe that open communication is key to providing the best possible dental care for your child.

Thank you again for choosing our practice for your child's dental needs, and we hope to see you and your family again soon.

reviews.comment: I have three children who all saw Dr. Maria for the first time yesterday. Firstly, being able to schedule all of them at the same time and on a Saturday was incredibly helpful. We were coming from a practice that could only ever manage to schedule one or two at a time, and trying to juggle time off work and remembering all of the dentist appointments was stressful. Our family of 5 was easily accommodated, the children were allowed to play and explore during their time there, and the dental assistants were very engaging and explained exactly what they were doing/would happen for the kids. Dr. Maria was quick, kind, very thorough, and never judgmental (which again- was a nice change from our former practice). We are looking forward to going back!

reviews.comment: I love this practice so much. Dr. Maria is one of the most caring, understanding, and polite doctors I have ever met. She saved my daughter Kiera's teeth many years ago after being on the bottle too long. She never once said I was a bad parent (which I felt like at the time) but instead gave me help on how to 1. Get her off the bottle and 2. Help with her teeth. Fast forward to today I have another daughter that was born severely preemie at 30 weeks. She has many health issues because of it along with swallowing problems among other things. She went above and beyond writing a letter that my daughter pediatrician requested on her findings to help get my child into the right specialist. If you are looking for a dentist that actually cares and listens to your concerns look no further than this practice!
reviews.response:
Dear Paige,

Thank you so much for taking the time to leave such a wonderful review about our practice, Dental Center Pediatrics! We are delighted to hear about your positive experiences with Dr. Maria and our team.

It is heartwarming to know that Dr. Maria provided you with the care and guidance you needed when faced with challenges concerning your daughter Kiera's teeth. We strive to create a caring and understanding environment for all our patients and their families.

Additionally, we appreciate the trust you have placed in us for your other daughter's dental needs. We are glad to have been able to assist with providing the necessary information for her specialist referral. 

Thank you for recognizing our commitment to truly listening and addressing your concerns. We are dedicated to delivering attentive and personalized care to all our patients.

Warm regards,

The Dental Center Pediatrics Team

reviews.comment: Dr. Rushil Patel is great, very professional, polite and patient with toddlers. reviews.response:
Dear Alexandra,

Thank you so much for taking the time to leave us such a positive review! We are thrilled to hear that Dr. Rushil Patel provided excellent care for your 3-year-old daughter during her root canals. We pride ourselves on our team's professionalism, politeness, and ability to work with toddlers.

We appreciate your trust in us, and we're glad that we were able to meet your expectations by using laughing gas for the procedure instead of anesthesia. Our priority is to ensure a comfortable experience for our patients, especially the little ones.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please don't hesitate to reach out to us at (508) 830-1212. Thank you for recommending Dental Center Pediatrics!

Best regards,
[Your Name]

reviews.comment:
My boys first visit to the dentist was my dentist office after my son had a filling done there. I quickly learned not everyone can work on kids as a profession. They mostly see adults and with me fine but, I wanted better for my boys.

My friend takes her two boys around the same age as mine to Dental Center Pediatrics, she said good things so I figured I'd give it a try. I have a two and a four year old and have been going for about a year. They are patient and caring. My oldest had images done that showed he needed a lot of dental work. After weighing my options he was put under for his dental work. I was hesitant, confused and stressed. I was met with a patience and fully explained everything to my comfort and made decisions. Any reason I would put less stars I was so happy with the resolutions. My son's both had cleanings and although not completely cooperative they do not lose tempers, cool or force my boys. I appreciate it and them. I would recommend them.

My daughter wasn’t scared at all, they explained everything they were doing to the both of us. They were very attentive and gave me tips and tricks for healthy brushing with a toddler. Overall, it was a great experience and my daughter left with a positive mindset about the dentist.
I always have a great experience at this location. Everyone is always friendly and welcoming. My boys are always treated good and they are very quick to call you in. Thank you guys !
All staff are great with our kids!! While in the dental chair, everything is explained and the kids know what to expect before any work begins.
5 yr old “she was careful and I like playing in the waiting room.”

Switched my kid’s dentist to here recently, and I am so happy I did. The entire office is kid oriented, tv screens at each chair to keep kids occupied, kid painted scenery on the walls, and the staff is just amazing. We didn’t have a long wait, the assistants and dentist kept me and my 5 year old well informed. The office was clean and neat as well! They gift the kids with a balloon at the end (which is a huge hit)!

We switched over a few months back because of reviews and their hours. Dr. Maria has been great with each visit and office staff effective. My son underwent surgery last week and we were relatively pleased at the process. My son is doing great now :)
Love this office!!  All are very professional, get us in on time, and have great hours!
Dr Maria is wonderful. We are new patients and are very happy with this office. My kids don't mind going to the dentist at all!
We just swtiched over to this office and it is wonderful!  Great place for kids!!  Wish they also treated adults!
Dr. Maria is awesome!

My son chipped his front permanent tooth pretty bad this morning (Sunday) I called the emergency number and his tooth was fixed that day! Every time I've taken my kids to the facility everyone is very curtious and prompt. Would highly recommend!

My son had to have dental surgery, put under general anesthesia in Boston. DCP handled all the paperwork, all the calls. All i needed to do was wait for the hospital to call and go down. DCP made the surgery easy, less anxieties overall. thank you so much from Jason and our whole family 👪
reviews.response: Thank you very much for your trust Amber!! Doctor Maria and our office works hard for our litlle patients to give them the best dental experience!! 🦷🦷🦷

reviews.comment: I have been bringing my 3 year old to Dental Center Pediatrics for about a year. I’ve always had a good experience. They are great with his wiggles and his giggles. But after this week I am recommending to everyone I know because of the experience I had with Dr. Maria Kritsineli in an emergency situation. It was outstanding and I am so thankful for her. This past week I had an unfortunate event happen to my 10 month old. The end result being a chipped tooth and a panicked call to the office at 8:45pm. I was expecting to leave a message and get a call in the morning, but I actually was able to call Dr. Kritsineli’s cell and send her a photo of the tooth. She explained thoroughly what to do and answered all of my questions during our phone call as well as the visit with her. I really can’t thank her and her team enough for making this experience a little less stressful than it already was!
Dr. Maria really yook care of my sons teeth issues. I recommend this office!

My son is 5, and I have been bringing him to see Dr. Maria for almost 2 years now! He recently had a filling, and had the best experience! He is a very energetic and curious little boy and Dr.Maria and her team did an amazing job putting him at ease in the chair, and explaining everything they were going to do! He also loves the video games in the waiting room! I highly recommend bringing your little ones here!!
Dental hygienist, Deanne, was AMAZING with my anxious kiddo. My son was a little nervous about his appointment today but that faded fast thanks to her! Deanne - you’re the best :)

I can not put into words how above and beyond Dr. Maria and her staff go for my kids. I could not imagine telling them that we were changing dentists! We had others in the past elsewhere and though they were pediatric dentist offices they do NOT rise to the level of care that you will get here! From the reception at the desk, the waiting room , the beautiful murals on every wall in every room, to the true comfort and kindness of the whole staff making your child and you feel at ease is amazing!! Just go there and take a look around and you will understand why I recommend them to every parent I know!
The staff are so sweet and are great with children! My daughter is very shy and they know how to make her feel comfortable and enjoys going there!
Great atmosphere and friendly staff. I've been taking my son here since he was 2 and 3 years later, still the same friendly and caring service.

Ok- Wow- this place is amazing! Honestly, I'm very hard to please. I knew when I called for the appointment it was going to be great! Just the whole atmosphere was inviting & warm. You would never think it was a dentist office. The front desk staff, to the dental assistants right to the dentist herself! I wish all my boys came here. I may just have to switch.
My son who is 4 1/2 & not the biggest fan of the dentist, was happy & without fear! That's a great place!
Highly impressed! Very friendly staff, great with the kids. Recommend to everyone.

We have a wonderful experience every time we visit our favorite dentist(7 years). Dr. Kritsineli is very knowledgeable and sweet. My 3 girls absolutely love her. I can’t thank her enough for being so patient and nice with my kids.

We were visiting family when my 16 month old fell and had an dental emergency right before bedtime.  I called Dr. Kritsineli and she kindly got us in to be seen the next morning.  She was so tender with our baby and explained extensively what the next steps should be.  If I did not live so far away, I would be going to this practice for everything!  Do not look further for your kids, choose Dr. Kritsineli and the amazing staff.
Great office, kind staff, and easy to schedule!

Great experience for kids! My son is 3 and my daughter is 18 months and they love going here! Only problem is Everytime I need to schedule an appointment both receptionists say they only schedule til 2 because they close mon-fri at 3...but when I look online that is untrue they do stay open later. Scheduling is the only difficult part.
Always a great experience, they're so good with my toddler.
Everyone in the practice is so nice and caring.  They are always explaining what work they are doing and my kids are never scared.  We love Dental Center Pediatrics!

I take my daughter to this dentist and have since the first. She is 3 so I figured going to the dentist would be a struggle. But I have no complaints about anything. The staff is amazing and excellent with kids. The whole place is targeted specifically for kids with video games, and big comfy bean bag chairs in the waiting room, to toys and ballons as incentive after their appointment is finished. They also remind you of your appointment via text and communicate with you beforehand to remind you of upcoming appointments faithfully. They are very accommodating and work with you to make appointments based on your own schedule and availability.

Very kid friendly environment!! The Ladies in the reception area even helped keep an eye on my toddler while I filled out the new patient paperwork.  The hygienist and the dentist were both excellent with him and my little one was comfortable and relaxed for the whole visit!
Compassionate and caring. Very trustworthy and respectful. Always tells the patient and parent what’s going on. I recommend highly.
Friendly and knowledgeable team who are great with my daughter
It was my granddaughters first visit she had a great experience they also were very helpful while I filled out all the forms
My little guy's first dental appointment was a breeze. Friendly staff and overall good experience.

This office is a 20-minute drive for us, but I wouldn't do it if it wasn't worth it!  Dr. Maria is awesome!  She explains everything thoroughly to me AND my child.  Staff is friendly and helpful to wade through the dental insurance. Highly recommend this office!
I have a very satisfied experience every time my kids have an appointment at dental center pediatric

Dr. Kritsineli is the Best and our Favorite Dentist ever, and we’ve had quite a few before we finally found her seven years ago! She is always sweet and professional. All my three girls love her and always leave the office happy.

I was referred here when my little one needed oral surgery and I have to say they have been nothing but amazing. I just switched my children over from the previous dentist who referred us, although they were great as well. But they just know what they are doing with children at Dental Center Pediatrics!!!!
Awesome team of professionals . They are great with the children they provide care to. Highly recommend.
My daughter actually gets excited to go to the Dentist. It is a kids playland with friendly, knowledgeable and caring staff. 💗😃😍
Absolutely LOVE this office and how great they are with my daughter!
I honestly can't say enough positive things about Dr. Maria and her staff. My daughter absolutely LOVES going to the dentist because of them!!!.

My son has been a patient for close to five years now. After suffering an injury at daycare when he was two, we had our first visit. He loves going there, goes in by himself and has never been afraid of the dentist! All the staff are wonderful, receptionists, hygienists and doctors. I hope he never knows the anxiety of going to the dentist as many of us experience. One of the best choices I've made!
They make going to the dentist an easy experience on my kids (and myself!) Def recommend.
My son feels comfortable there.  He is usually very uncooperative when it comes to the dentist, but they have helped him come a long way for the exams. Im very grateful
Great experience- very organized, great dentist

I love dental center pediatrics! The dentist is alwaya so nice to my son and they have a lot for the kids to keep them entertained. I have never had to wait more than a couple minutes for an appointment and the appointment itself is quick which is great with a toddler. Highly reccomend them!
Great staff and doctors! Always accommodating and helpful. My girls actually get excited about going to the dentist.
Love love love this office.... Everyone here is so nice can’t say enough about them all. So I’m not going to what I am going to say is that I would recommend them to everyone and I have
reviews.response: Thank you, Lindsay. We are touched and thankful for your review!!!!!

reviews.comment: Outstanding experience! Highly recommend!!

We had a wonderful experience at Dental Center Pediatrics! All staff members were friendly & helped greatly to put my son at ease. After his first appointment there he said “I love my new dentist! Can I go back tomorrow?”

Wonderful!
Very friendly, caring and give you peace of mind. 
All staff are friendly and respectful.highly recommend
Wonderful atmosphere!  Everyone from the office staff to the hygienist and the dentist are knowledgeable and friendly and great with children.
We drive 2 hours each way to see Dr. Kritsineli for our daughter. Need we say anything else?
My kids love the staff and atmosphere of the Dental office. Even when they have to have work done they always are eager to go for the next visit.
Everyone was super nice and made my 3 year olds first visit to the dentist so easy!
Great pediatric dentistry team! Always impressed!

Was very worried about how my son was going to handle getting a cavity filled today due to past time wasn't so good on his party but he was amazing and Dr Maria and her staff was absolutely great with him.  I can't say enough Thankyou to Dr Maria and her staff
Dr Kritsineli and staff are wonderful, she makes sure going to the dentist is a good experience for children. The staff is warm and welcoming and the hours are fantastic.
Great staff. My 2.5 year and feels comfortable there and isn't afraid to get her teeth cleaned.

My son has been going to Dr. Maria and her wonderful staff for 4+ years. They are ALWAYS so happy, eager to help, and patient. I cannot emphasize "they are patient" enough. My son has sensory issues and with all the sounds, lights, flashes, etc he can break down a bit. They work so hard to remind him that they are willing to wait until he is ready....and I appreciate that.
We LOVE the staff at Dental Center Pediatrics!
Excellent group!

Very friendly and helpful staff and Dr. Maria was wonderful with my son - who can tend to be a nervous little guy. I found this place by recommendation from a friend and would most definitely recommend this place to others!
Dr. Maria, Dr. Karissa and all the staff are absolutely awesome. I take all my kids here and so do my friends. They treat your kids like their own and the work is excellent!
Always great!

My children have been going for over a year. They are wonderful they really put the kids at ease. You never have to wait more than a few minutes. The office is very clean and bright happy place. I would highly recommend Dental Center Pediatrics
My 3.5 year old has been going here since she was about 20 months old.  Both the hygentist and dentist are always so patient and gentle. Highly recommend

From the front desk to Dr. Maria I couldn’t ask for more. The front desk took care of everything including scheduling and insurance verification for 3 different insurances. It was such a project but she did it patiently and was so nice. Dr. Maria was amazing. She explained everything in great detail and found something in my 10 year old that no other dentist had picked up. So knowledgeable and very sweet with my daughter. Took a great deal of time with teaching and explanation. Highly recommend!
Always a great experience!

Dentist is a mom that gets toddlers
My toddler had to have a cavity filled, a first for her, and both of us were a little nervous (me more than her, to be honest)...
The staff was friendly and realistic, spoke to my daughter like they really cared. They have Tv's with kids movies over all the chairs, and have cutesy names for all the procedures. Overall, they made a tough situation, easy.

Alleviated fear and hard to numb tooth
Dr Kritsineli was wonderful! She was able to help alleviate my daughters fear of feeling pain in a tooth that just wouldn’t numb. She was able to numb it and fix it without issue. Very knowledgeable and help moms fears as well! Thank you!

High equality service and friendly staff
It's always to see the same staff and awesome service. It's always nice to see Dr. Maria.

Awesome bedside manners
I have a 5 yo autistic boy. We were here for his 6month check up. Everything was great until the woman brought out the spin brush for the fluoride. My son, started to have a bit of a fit and the woman knew what to do. She was absolutely amazing ! She assured my son she wasn't going to hurt him, she let him hold the tool and feel it spin. After all that, he calmed down and all was well. She took him to get a few prizes and a balloon. My son was in heaven. They're really great here. I would DEFINATELY recommend them.

We love Dr Maria! With three children (two having braces) we see her a lot!! Always so friendly and caring. It may sound funny but she’s like part of the family now. My children all smile when they see her. The whole staff is wonderful.

Highly impressed!
I have been to many dentists over the years between myself and 5 children. I usually end up being a one time patient due to the lack of compassion, care or patience. 3 of my children recently had checkups and my 2 boys who both have dissabilities, 1 of which is on the Autism spectrum were treated with the absolute best care. Dr. Koster and her assistant were so kind, positive and patient and my 8 yr old ASD son has severe anxiety and you never would have known it. He was able to ask questions...a lot of them...and have them answered. I never felt like the Dr. was annoyed with my son or minimized his 8 yr old concerns. To have my son sit so calmly, not cry or have an episode while he had his sealants put in is a testament to this practice. Dental Center Pediatrics know how to work with children...all kinds of children and I am so happy to have found them. Even though they are a 45 min drive for us it is worth it to have a Dentist and staff that care about what they do!

So awesome!
The best

Great service, friendly staff
I have a 2 and 4 yo. We have been going to Dental Central Pediatrics for 1 year now. My children have met with both Dr Kritsineli and Dr Koster and both have been excellent with my young children. The hygienist are wonderful too. The front staff is friendly and efficient. The office and tools appear to be clean/sterile-which is very important!
I will continue to go and also recommend this pediatric dental center to others.
Kenny was as pleased as ever...thank you

Best Pedi Dentist around!!
My kids love it there! Doctors and staff are very patient and caring. They always go out out there way to make the child feel comfortable. Best pediatric dentist around! Definitely recommended!
Our doctor today was great!! It was our first visit (18 months) and it was made so easy by the staff.

Flouride
Everything went quickly and smoothly for all my children! We love going here!!!

Great visit as usual
Great visit today, thank you for being so good with the kids, you work hard to make them as comfortable as you can and I can say, it is very much appreciated by the parents.

Staff are extremely professional, efficient, and knowledgeable.
I absolutely am so impressed with Dental Center Pediatrics. We are new patients and I can honestly say that your group has gone above and beyond for us. The professionalism and the knowledge that you have met us with is superb.
Thank you!

Amazing!
Thank you for another wonderful visit to the dentist. You all are amazing at what you do.

Another great experience!
We had another great experience at Dental Center Pediatrics yesterday.
Both boys got there 5 minutes early for their appt. Although there were several people in the waiting room, we hardly had time to sit down before they were each called in. The communication is always great regarding insurance, finances, treatment, and recommendations. The staff are friendly, professional, and informative. My kids like going to the dentist - That says it all!

Emergency Visit
Dr. Maria was great! When our two year old had an accident and needed to be seen on a holiday weekend, Dr. Maria opened up the office so our daughter could be seen. She personally checked in with us leading up to our visit and was very patient with our daughter during her visit (it was our daughter's first visit too). She was also very good at reassuring/comforting us, the parents. My daughter loved the balloon at the end of the visit. We were very grateful that Dr. Maria took the time out of her weekend to help a new patient!

Professional and super friendly
Today was my son's first ever appointment and he did so awesome! Thanks to the super friendly and very informative hygienist and the very knowledgeable dentist they helped make his experience so positive and easy. The front staff were also extremely friendly and very engaging with my son. Made the whole experience an easy one!!!
My son loves going to the dentist ! Great dentists and staff .

Very friendly and VERY patient
I love these girls. They are super kind, patient and care about the kids. Love the atmosphere to make the kiddos feel safe, and fun. My ONLY complaint is.
I wish they would put in a half door. So, the little ones don't constantly run down the hall ways or into other room.
Over all, I referred about 4 ppl here.

From the time you walk in the front door to the time you leave the staff is great. Excellent dentist that is great at what she does and is great with kids. The whole staff always goes above and beyond to make it a great experience for the kids and the parents.
My almost 3 year old's first visit so I was expecting the worst!!! Fallon and Dr. Karissa were amazing with Keegan!!! So happy that I choose here for his dental care!!

Awesome Dental Cleaning
My daughter received an awesome dental cleaning from Fallon! Thanks so much!
Very good with my son & very quick!

Love this dentist..always ready to help..my special needs daughter requires a lot of communication w.dentist due to being nonverbal and can't verbalize any pains and such. Dr.Kritsinelli is always trying to help and recommends the least invasive ways to give her the care she needs.

4 fillings for Caiden!
My son was seen today to have a number of fillings taken care of.....he is very afraid of needles....
The staff used nitrous very successfully with him.....overall the experience was great....everyone was very sweet with him and took very good care of him....I am very appreciative of that.....
The one recommendation I would make is especially for older kids like my son (almost 11) to give them a little more information before starting the process....talk them through what is going to happen in advance.....it felt like things moved very quickly at the beginning....there was a crying child in the next room and he was anxious....once the process started the doctor told Caiden what was going to happen next but I think a little of advance talk prior to nitrous, etc. would have been great.
Thank you again for taking such good care of him.
Always great service!

reviews.comment: I just had an appointment today for my five year old. Everyone here is so friendly and they make my daughter very comfortable. The whole enviroment is just for kids and really makes it enjoyable for them! Games in the waiting room, television while they get a cleaning or filling and prizes when they finish. The office is very clean and professional like it should be . The dentist talks her through everything and in a special way for a small child to understand and feel like they are doing something fun for their teeth and make them proud of themselves . So instead of being scared like at her old dentist she now likes going and its all because of this place, which inturn makes it alot easier for me when it's time to brush,floss and rinse daily and for her check ups. I wish I could give all the women here a huge thankyou for what they do for children. I would give them 10 stars if I could. I seriously recommend this place to anyone with children whether they like the dentist or not they will love it after going. I couldn't be happier with my decision in bringing her here.

reviews.comment: I like how they try to work round your schedule. The office staff are very friendly, and willing to help. It's nice to see happy smiling, energetic faces that care about their kids, and making them comfortable.

reviews.comment:
Compassionate, caring and kind
My daughter is 3. She has been going to this dental practice for over a year now. She needed her 1st filling. I was nervous as to how she would do. Dr. Carissa was amazing. Patient, kind, gentle. Made my daughter (and I) feel relaxed during the whole procedure. She used notorious oxide (laughing gas) and kept saying kind word to my daughter. I was so impressed. Knowing the love that the staff has for their patients is unbelievable. We love it there!!
reviews.response:
Thank you so much for taking the time to write a complimentary review. We strive to have the children be as comfortable as possible while their dental health is being taken care of. We are lucky to have Dr. Karissa who is so caring and attentive as a member of the Dental Pediatrics team here. We appreciate your feedback and
any referrals you can make to your family or acquaintances. Thanks again

reviews.comment: Our experiences with Dental Center Pediatrics have always been extremely positive. It's easy to get there, and easy to park. The office is beautiful, accommodating both children and adults very nicely. They've been professionally decorated, with a hand painted aquarium theme which flows throughout the office areas. There are big, fun beanbag chairs for the kids in the center of the reception room, as well as a television, video games, children's books and magazines. Adults are seated around the beanbags in elegant, comfortable chairs, with plenty of neatly arranged magazines. The reception staff is professional, kind, warm, and efficient. We've rarely had to wait more than five minutes to go in. Parents can be with and see their children through the entire visit. X-rays are done quickly, and a full orbital (?) machine is readily available so that you can bring whole mouth X-rays to orthodontists. Dental chairs and equipment are new and immaculate. Children watch a movie while the work is being done. The hygienists are professional and focused. They take the time to answer all questions and are helpful in motivating kids to brush and floss. Dr. Kritsinelu is perhaps the kindest, warmest professional I've ever met. She puts my child completely at ease. She is conservative, doing what is necessary, and only that. She takes the time to explain to parents and children what is going on, keeping everyone relaxed and informed. Kids leave with the usual goodie bag of a new toothbrush etc., as well as a prize. They have a great appointment reminder system, and do a great job with scheduling. Dr. "Maria" (Kritsinelu) has done a perfect job of providing excellent dental care in a comfortable environment with a terrific support team. I only wish she could treat adults!
reviews.response:
Thank you so much for a very detailed and extremely complimentary review. We are happy to hear that your child feels at ease here since we strive to have the children have positive visits when their dental health is being cared for. We are also very fortunate to have Dr. Maria Kritsineli. Dr. Kritsineli loves her patients and her work and this is
reflected by the care she provides to her patients.  Its a team effort here and everyone here takes pride in their work. We appreciate any referral you can give to your family and acquaintances.Thank you again for taking the time  to write an extensive review which demonstrates that a lot of thought was put into it. We look
forward to providing future dental care for your child. Take care.

reviews.comment:

reviews.comment:
Always wonderful and compassionate people
My son has been going to this office for a few years now. Everyone I have met has always been wonderful and compassionate, especially since my child is differently abled.
I was; however, (1) disappointed about the dental restorative care. It would be a better practice if they treated it like adults. No one called about the co-pays or discuss my child's ability to sit or what extra precautions could be used to minimalize the scare.
(2) I knew we would owe something, but was shocked to find out how much and what I would need to pay out of pocket because something was not covered by insurance. That should not happen. Those expectations should be laid out before arrival.
(3) The hygienist did not complete the xrays and the doctor did not know she needed to take xrays until after all the numbing medicines had been administrated.
Upon finding my child could not tolerate an office visit to handle his dental needs, the doctor was so compassionate and empathized with me. I was not expecting to need to organize a surgery into an already busy summer. But the office staff explained things right away. Gave me documents, and told me to call them with any and all questions.
Wish that was there approach for the office visit too. Perhaps some of the difficulty could have been avoided on both the doctor's, office, and my behalf.
GREAT
Everyone in the office was great. I also loved the murals on the walls.
reviews.response: Thank you so much for your kind comments. We are glad that your trip to our office was a good one. We also love the murals. They are like a mini escape.

reviews.comment:
Wonderful Staff & Doctor
From the minute we walked in the door we were attended to by a staff that truly understands and obviously enjoys working with children. The hygenists worked quickly and calmly to get my son's x-rays done and keep him comfortable, and the doctor did the same. Dr. Maria needed to extract my son's tooth and she was always careful to keep all the tools out of the child's line of sight, and work very quickly to get the procedure done. The only time he was upset (not from pain or any procedure she was doing, rather just being scared of having to breathe through his nose- go figure) she calmly told him just tell me what makes you scared and I will fix it, no problem. And when he was still scared she stopped and said no problem, we won't make him do it- we have to do whatever he is comfortable with. I can say I wish this was the dental experience I had as a child, and that I could go see her myself! Thank you Dr. Maria & all the staff!!!!
reviews.response: We are so happy that the experience went well for you both. Thank you for the kind words!

allReviews.comment: 3 kids were patients for about 7 years. My youngest needed to be put under to have tooth extractions. We were scheduled 6 months out and put on a waiting list. 2 weeks out they called and left a voicemail that the appointment would be cancelled if they didn’t receive the paperwork. I called the next day and was told the appointment was actually cancelled because “lots of people are waiting for these appointments” and they didn’t have the paperwork. So the appointment was cancelled before even speaking with me and gave me no opportunity to call back because I called the next day. Same exact situation happened 1 week before the appointment. When I had already sent the paperwork twice and verified with the pediatrician that the paperwork was sent. I called back begging for the appointment back because my pediatrician sent the paperwork and I resent it again and had already taken the day off of work. Got the appointment back. Thursday before the appointment I got a voicemail the appointment was cancelled again. I called back and was told secondary health insurance wasn’t active and I need to pay the copay before the procedure on Monday (2 days, $500). I called the secondary insurance and was told everything was fine. I called the office back and they said it wasn’t. I called the insurance again and they said there was nothing wrong. I called the office back and was told the computer can’t verify it so they needed the money and the appointment was cancelled because I could not pay and I could reschedule when I had the copay. Office manger called me and told me the health insurance website was down and that is why they couldn’t verify it. The appointment was cancelled 3 times on me, twice the week before the procedure before even actually speaking with me. When I was told the appointments are in high demand, I reminded the manager that my son had been on the waitlist for 6 months without a notification of any cancellations. This was extremely stressful as a single mother and I told them to cancelled the procedure and to take my kids off of their panel. I always recommended this office but for the last 5 years has gone down hill. The secretaries are rude even when going into the office.

allReviews.comment:
I brought by two boys in for their dental cleanings on Thursday 6/19. My 3 year old had a great experience with a very kind and patient hygienist (didn't catch her name), that we have had before. I am hesitant to write a negative review, but I feel this is important information. 

My older son had Tammy for a hygienist. We have had her one other time. Both times I found that she seemed very irritated and frustrated. The first time I figured maybe it was a bad day. However, the second time was the same issue. My son has generalized anxiety and is especially anxious to go to medical settings. In fact, he had been very nervous to go to his appointment that morning. Tammy seemed very irritated that he wasn't brushing thoroughly enough and wasn't flossing. I agree with the assessment and it has been something I have tried working with him on, but I'm guessing that many children do not brush adequately or floss daily. Instead of being blunt and irritated with instructions, I think it would be more beneficial to show a child how to brush adequately with patience and kindness. When my son was tensing and having difficulty keeping his mouth open, she again acted irritated. As someone with dental anxiety myself, you want to feel comfortable with the person who is working in your mouth.

I completely understand that working with children is not easy as I am a kindergarten teacher myself. However, when you enter a field specializing in children, you should expect that you will deal with children and they will not always be easy to work with. I take my children to a pediatric dentist so that there is more patience and specialized care for children and this was not our experience.
allReviews.response:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback about your recent visit with your sons. We sincerely appreciate your honest assessment of your experience, especially regarding your older son's appointment. We acknowledge your concerns about the interaction with Tammy and understand the importance of creating a comfortable environment for children, particularly those with anxiety.

We are committed to addressing your concerns and will be reviewing this internally to ensure all our staff members provide the patient, kind, and specialized care you expect from a pediatric dental practice. Your feedback is valuable in helping us improve our services.

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss this matter further and address any additional concerns you may have. Please feel free to contact us at (508) 830-1212 so we can better understand your experience and work towards a resolution.

We really appreciate Rachel  for taking the time out to share your experience with us.

Cheers,

Dental Center Pediatrics

allReviews.comment:
As you know, we have been patients of your practice for quite some time, I have three young children who have been coming to Dental Center Pediatrics for many years.  In the past nine years we have never had a cleaning appointment in which all three of my children have been able to go in for cleaning at the same time as there is typically only two hygienists and two chairs available for cleanings. 

My wife had my youngest son Erik dismissed early from his reading tutor so he could attend his 4:30 dental appointment. She was bringing him to the dental appointment and I left work early to bring my other two sons. My wife assured me that in the past, only two children were able to go in for cleanings at the same time so if she was a few minutes behind in arriving it shouldn't be a problem as he would be there before a full cleaning was completed for either of the other two brothers. However, due to Erik's dental anxiety he struggled to transition into the car. While waiting for Erik to arrive, within a five minute period, I was asked three times when he would arrive. I explained the situation and even showed the hygenist our family tracking app, so she could see that they were at the traffic light outside the building. The hygenist, I don't know her name, was rude as a starting point and only escalated from there each time she asked me when Erik would arrive. Erik eventually arrived nine or ten minutes late, at which point neither of his brothers were finished with their cleanings.

When my wife arrived with Erik, she was chastised for being late in an extremely rude and condescending tone in front of Erik who is already struggling with anxiety related to coming to the dentist. I have see a lot of inappropriate behavior from adults in my life, but this incident was over the top by far. I noticed that there were several signs around the office to remind customers that rude behavior towards staff will not be tollerated. I hope that there are several signs like this in the staff areas, that direct the staff not to be rude to customers. Going forward, I would like to ensure that none of my children have future appointments with this hygenist, that is if we can even convince Erik to return to Dental Center Pediatrics.

I hope to hear back from someone at Dental Center Pediatrics, to confirm receipt of this message as when messages are sent to these general mailboxes they often go ignored.

allReviews.comment:
If I could give a zero I would, I’ve been taking my kids here for over 2 years and up until I had Dr Maria, I was very happy with this place. A couple weeks ago I was told my daughter (who is 7) had a cavity that had to be pulled and that they had to give me a referral because they couldn’t pull it there at the office in case they couldn’t get it all. The place they referred me to has nothing until November. A few days later she lost a piece of that same tooth. I made an apt and brought her in again. Dr Maria then tells me as my daughter is laying in the chair that she can pull it right then, has me sign paperwork for the laughing gas, and naturally my daughter gets very upset and scared considering she just heard all of this and wasn’t expecting that. Not only did neither her or the dental assistant not even attempt to calm her, they did the opposite. The dental assistant said not one word. Dr Maria yelled at my daughter to not scream like that. I left with my daughter and said I would call them (I was desperate to have this tooth pulled because my daughter was in a lot of pain) I call them a half hour later and had to fight for my daughter to get another appointment to the point they had Dr Maria herself on the phone to tell me “ I have next Thursday only and this is her last chance” I said okay. We came that Thursday, my daughter again cried at first but not nearly as bad as the first time and just kept asking questions about what was going to happen, Maria didn’t answer one of her questions , she sat on her stool and rolled her eyes with an “I told you so” look . The dental assistant this time was very nice and showed my daughter the laughing gas mask on a teddy’s bear face etc. which I was very appreciative of. After 15 mins Maria says to my daughter, you need to stop crying, you’ve wasted enough time, then puts the chair back up says this was a half hr apt and we would have to wait til her tooth naturally falls out piece by piece and that it wouldn’t last til our November apt. My daughter started saying I’ll do it I’ll do it and Maria still told me no so I said no we’re getting this done she already is in pain I cannot wait for more of her tooth to come out so she’s in even more pain. In the end Dr. Maria told me I was talking to her like I talked to my daughter and was bickering with me over me advocating for my daughter and for her to just do her job. Maria pulled the tooth and I apologized for it getting so hectic (which I thought was very kind of me considering how rude she was being to
Myself and my 7 year old)…fast forward to two weeks later, I get a letter stating she will no longer accept my daughter at their dentist anymore due to my behavior. As the owner of this pediatric dentist, you’d think she’d have some compassion for not only the mothers attempting to console and calm their child, never mind some for the actual child. I am beyond disgusted with this whole situation and just how unprofessional she truly was to say the least.

I have no idea how this Dental center got their license to become a pediatric dentist because they are far from having the patients for our kids. My niece had to get her tooth removed and she was very scared and upset and instead of the Dentist comforting her and making her feel more comfortable she was very unprofessional and showed she was aggravated that she couldn’t rush to do her job and get it over with had absolutely no patience for my 7 year old niece, instead of doing her job said it will fall out on her own knowing that would bother her when she goes to eat anything for weeks and how painful that can be. Then sent a letter saying my niece is no longer allowed at their office and needs to find a new dentist. I do not recommend bringing your kids here they do not have the patience and can’t handle it. Very unprofessional.

allReviews.comment: I came in today with my three year old to have his teeth checked for cavities as I saw some coloration on his molars and was concerned. His older brother had complications that could have been prevented if caught early enough and I really want to avoid that with him. I came in and the tech and Dr.Patel looked quickly over the teeth making some suggestions. Literally not even five minutes they looked like they were done. The tech told me she would "show me how to brush his teeth" it was then she noticed a cavity in the groove of his teeth. I feel like I was rushed and it wasn't thoroughly looked at the first time. I mentioned several times about imaging to make sure nothing else is going on. Thankfully Dr.Patel came back and we talked about a game plan. He even mentioned imagining and I said I agree and the tech interrupted saying he didn't sit well for his cleaning in January (per the notes). I personally just gave birth and wasn't present for that visit however I feel like we could have atleast tried. I've never had a problem or bad experience. Dr.Patel was awesome he explained things thoroughly and listened to me. I however felt rushed and frustrated. I will continue to push for imaging of teeth so we have them. Also I want to be sure something is not there that we can not see with our eyes. I don't think asking for imaging is out of the ordinary when it is standard practice. The tech had a lot of information on not feeding my son crackers, such as Ritz or chips. I don't think she is suited for pediatric dentistry. Her delivery was a bit harsh although I took all the suggestions and advice I feel like it was unsolicited and unnecessary.
allReviews.response:
Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We're sorry to hear that you felt rushed and frustrated during your visit. We understand how important it is to have your child's dental health thoroughly examined, especially given your concerns and your older child's complications.

We're glad to hear that Dr. Patel was able to provide you with a thorough explanation and listened to your concerns. However, we apologize that you felt that the initial examination was not thorough enough. We agree that imaging is an important part of dental care, and we apologize for any miscommunication that may have occurred regarding the need for imaging during your visit.

Regarding the advice given by the dental assistant, we appreciate your feedback and take it seriously. We will be sure to review this with our staff to ensure that our recommendations are always delivered in a caring and supportive manner.

We understand that your child's dental health is of the utmost importance, and we appreciate your trust in our practice. We will work hard to ensure that your future visits are more comfortable, thorough, and enjoyable. Thank you again for your feedback, and please do not hesitate to reach out to us if you have any further concerns or questions.

Regards,

allReviews.comment: I was referred to Dr Kristineli by Carver Smiles because she needed a cavity filled and he didn’t have an appointment available until March. Dr Kristineli was not only rude, but she told me she would have to take her to the hospital to have her put under general anesthesia, extract the tooth and do work on multiple surrounding teeth. None of this was mentioned by her dentist. Only a filling and I was referred to her because she uses laughing gas to help. Decided to get a second opinion at New England smiles in Plymouth, who said she only needed a cavity filled. There was no need for extraction or other tooth work. The second dentist was surprised Dr Kristineli was saying she needed to do all that work. Makes me feel like she just looks for reasons to spend people’s insurance money to pocket.

Our experience was horrifying. I will never allow my children into your practice again. I will be reported Dr Patel the the  Massachusetts board of dentistry. My daughter Caitlin went into have a cavity filled. When Dr Patel told us how the procedure would go he mentioned put some "medicine" on the tooth. When I asked what the "medicine" was he said oh it's just a liner. When I asked him what the "liner" was. He said oh it's "fluoride". My daughter who is immunocompromised and has multiple drug allergies and sensitives no longer is getting fluoride. Fluoride is a neurotoxin. The FDA has never done safety studies on fluoride and children. I specifically said no fluoride liner. Dr Patel immediately said "well if she has pain then we would have to refer you out to someone else" I said I understand that no fluoride! Dr Patel proceeded to drill her tooth, apply the white filing, and then right in front of me applied beautifil flow fluoride liner! When I asked what he was doing he looked at me and said oh it's the filing. I have pictures of the fluoride container. I have the exact information on what this product is. It is a self releasing fluoride. I can not tell how absolutely offended I am. This dentist performed a procedure in front of me when I said no and did it anyways. Someone better call me immediately. I am leaving your practice. I will post this on goggle if this incident isn't deal with and appropriate disciplinary actions have been taken.

allReviews.comment:
Dr. Lu and Grace appeared to be very curt and rude today, which is not consistent with their usual interactions with us.
They declined to let Fallon have 2 colors of elastics on her braces, given that they have done it many times before and that it takes an extra 10 seconds to grab a different color I was very upset especially considering the amount of money orthodontia costs.
Additionally, after 6 months of not having to turn the pallet expander, I was practically berated for having thrown out her key for turning. When I asked if I could have one, I was made to feel like I was asking for a thousand dollar item. I even offered to buy one myself if I could be told where to get them. Only to be told they "special order" them. 
I almost called after I left to complain about this poor treatment but decided not to.
I assure you if we have an experience like this again we will be finding new dentist and orthodontist. 
I hope it was just 2 people having a bad day. But rudeness to a 9 year old is not something that should be or need to be tolerated.

We have been coming to your office for several years now and have never had a bad experience but, we had an experience yesterday and I just wanted to let you know about it.  I was waiting in the waiting room and after the hygienist was finished with my sons teeth she just walked him to the waiting room and dumped him out there without making eye contact with me, without saying anything to me and without giving me any info about how his appointment went.  I had to go into the hallway and call to her to ask how things went to which she replied “fine I think”. When I asked if there was any sort of follow up or info we needed she said “I don’t know, do you want me to ask the dentist?” I said yes so after a few minutes the dentist did come out.  I was surprised to see it wasn’t Dr. Maria but a new doctor who I had never met.  He was great after meeting him and spent a few minutes talking to us about the appointment so that did make me feel better but it would have been nice to have him introduce himself as we have never met him before.  Typically when my children have had their cleanings the hygienist or dentist spends a minute or two with me letting me know how the visit went and if there is anything I need to know.  Especially now that my kids are older and go to the room alone. 

My daughter had her cleaning at the same time as my son and her hygienist came out after, spoke to me about how it went, gave us some feedback and was overall lovely- and that is what we are used to.  As I said, we have never had a bad experience before but if our next visit has the same lack of communication then we will be looking for a new dentist office.
allReviews.response:

Went in to get SDF done on ONE of my child’s teeth. Was assured by the doctor the day before the procedure that the rest of his cavities were minor and they’d keep an eye on them every 3 months. Only the very back right molar needed SDF, as the rest were not that bad.

We go in for SDF treatment the next day and the lady put SDF on FOUR teeth! INCLUDING HIS FRONT TWO TEETH. My handsome boy with a beautiful smile now has two black front teeth.

To say I’m upset is an understatement. I am beyond mad about this. I told the lady to stop applying and asked her what she was doing? Told her my son was there to get ONE tooth done and she says “Huh, what do you mean? Why?” Like did she not read his chart or did the doctor not specify? Then she says “well in my opinion he should have them all done”. In your opinion?! YOU AREN’T MY SON’S DOCTOR!!!

Thanks for ruining my child’s pictures and potentially his social life for the next 7-10 years of his life without consulting me first.

allReviews.comment: Always super friendly great staff

The medical portion of the visit was amazing! I couldn’t believe how well my children handled this experience. The doctor and the nurse treated both of them with such care and compassion! They explained everything to them so well during the procedure. I believe it helped them feel so comfortable that they were able to cooperate and remain calm. 

My issue was the administrative team for both the first and second visit. Today when I checked both kids in they were so unwelcoming and unpleasant. There was literally zero effort put into being friendly. After the appointment I was handed a large unexpected bill and when I asked questions they weren’t able to answer them. I handed my card to her. so she knew I wasn’t leaving, and said I was going into the hallway to call insurance. She said I’d have to speak to her manager. I have spoken to the billing manager twice and we have the situation handled pending insurance finalizing the claim. After I got off the phone with the manager I noticed my sons mouth was bleeding. I said oh do you know if this is normal after this procedure and she said I don’t know you’ll have to ask them. I missed getting the nurses attention and she did end up getting the doctor for me. Something like “oh I’m not sure let me get the doctor” probably would have been a lot nicer. 

I’m shocked that I would have to even complain about this because a lot of it is common courtesy. They’re your front line, they’re the first people your patients talk to on the phone and they’re the first and last impression your patients have when they come in and leave your office. Not that it matters what kind of office it is but it’s specifically pediatrics and they should be kinder. 

Thank you!

I think dr. Maria is great and at least one of the dental hygienist his amazing, but the other hygienist was a bit rude and judgmental. She made me feel bad for certain things. Also the front dentist women Loretta wasn’t very personable and gave the same vibe. I always go back because dr. Maria is amazing

Dr. Maria is amazing!!!! All of my kids love to go to the dentist now. She has went above and beyond in providing a great service. Recently my daughter had to undergo general anesthesia for dental work. Dr. Maria made us and my daughter feel so comfortable and calmed all her fears. On another occasion, we were in a bind and needed emergency dental care over Easter weekend. We weren’t even patients yet but Dr. Maria called me back and saw us in the office. I will never forget how kind and thoughtful she was. I am so appreciative of her.
All of the staff at Dental Center Pediatrics have been very professional and very friendly.

allReviews.comment: My children had appointments in October of 2019 and they had fluoride which my insurance only covers once a year. I was charged $64 for my two children which I paid. Today I was told I have a balance because (according to the front desk staff) my insurance said I should have been charged $49 per child not $32 per child. I paid the balance although I think it is wrong. If my “balance” is almost 12 months old why haven’t I ever been notified prior to this? Also I know that insurance companies don’t dictate to the office what I pay. You charge a fee and the insurance either pays it or they don’t and I pay the balance. I realize that there are contractual write offs that happen between a practice and an insurance but this was straight forward. I know they don’t cover the 2nd fluoride treatment each year and I pay out of pocket at the time of the visit.  The whole situation seems very fishy and I’ll be contacting my insurance company for further explanation.

allReviews.comment:
This was my daughter’s (age 3) first visit to the practice. When we arrived, I was told that I needed my license, which I was unaware of. When I couldn’t find it, the intake woman was impatient and brusque with me, to the point where I was near tears. Even after I apologized for misplacing my license, she did not express understanding, patience, or offer any other means for getting it to her, though I finally offered to look through my photos to find one of my license. She was impatient while I looked through thousands of archived photos to find one. At no time did she express empathy or offer a kind word. I understand that, during the pandemic, things are more stressful, but they are more stressful for everyone, and kindness and understanding would have gone a long way.

On an unrelated note, I was surprised that there wasn’t a sticker or “prize box” offered after my daughter’s appointment. We were grateful for the kind treatment by the hygienist and good feedback from the doctor.

I have and will continue to recommend Dr. Kritsineli and the Dental Center Pediatric’s too my other friends and family. They are great! My four-year-old looks forward too and enjoys her dental visits believe it or not! I’m allowed to be with her the whole time. They have taken great care of her and her teeth. I have no complaints. They have new procedures and safety measures after the pandemic in their office. They re-opened with these new safety measures implemented as quickly as possible. I plan on bringing my daughter here for all her dental needs in the future.

It was not difficult to get an appointment, staff is friendly and assist with any questions you may have. It eased anxieties of visiting a dentist and walked us step by step. I will continue to take my children here.
