allReviews.comment: My children have been coming to Dental Center Pediatrics seeing Demi Kritsineli and staff- we are very pleased with the service and care everyone provides.  They explain everything to my children and answer all my questions.  My children don’t mind coming to the dentist :) I would recommend to my friends!

Today my daughter had her first dentist appointment. The dentist and the hygienist were so wonderful. They are very good with children. My daughter wasn’t scared at all, they explained everything they were doing to the both of us. They were very attentive and gave me tips and tricks for healthy brushing with a toddler. Overall, it was a great experience and my daughter left with a positive mindset about the dentist.
Jake said the person was nice but a little rough and when he got home he had a canker. I am not worried one bit, but wanted to mention his feedback.
I always have a great experience at this location. Everyone is always friendly and welcoming. My boys are always treated good and they are very quick to call you in. Thank you guys !
All staff are great with our kids!! While in the dental chair, everything is explained and the kids know what to expect before any work begins.

The front desk staff hardly made eye contact with me. Very cold and unwelcoming during check in. Didn’t like the vibe whatsoever. My daughter is 3. Difficult dental patient which I understand. Dentist didn’t say much but “floss her teeth”. Dental hygienists were nice enough.
5 yr old “she was careful and I like playing in the waiting room.”

My four year old son had an appointment for some fillings scheduled at 11:15. 

We arrived before 11:15 and we're not called back until 1145/1150. There was no explanation or I'm sorry. It's a dental center for kids. It should be understood that it is hard for little kids to wait. Especially for a filling and not a cleaning. It should be understood that most kids will feel anxious and waiting in the room for that long only adds to this anxiety. 

As soon as my son was brought back we heard screaming. Apparently the numbing agent was rubbed onto my sons gum and immediately after a needle was shot into his mouth. 
I was brought back as my son was having a hard time going through with the procedure and the dental assistant offered some sort of a sdf? To put over teeth to prolong the need for fillings but it would cost out of pocket... I then looked at the sheet and saw all the side effects and said absolutely not. The assistant didn't explain the paperwork or express any sympathy to the situation. When I was told by my husband how they put the needle in his mouth immediately I obviously questioned it and the assistants only response is that the numbing agent works immediately. 

I explained how last time they explained everything and waited to make sure he was ready. I also explained how as an adult I've never had a needle so quickly after the numbing agent. She didn't express any sympathy to the situation.  

I told my son the bad part was over and he would do a good job. Try to get one filling done. Well when they tried to place the rubber piece in his mouth to keep it open he was extremely upset and the person's response was "tell me what's wrong can you express yourself"".  He couldn't. He is 4, extremely upset, shy and sensitive.  

When checking out to reschedule my husband mentioned to Nick, who was the only understanding person throughout the whole ordeal, what happened and the woman next to him on the phone not even involved in the conversation had the audacity to excuse the numbing/needle fiasco with well when your backed up you can't make everyone backed up. We weren't even talking to her. 

There was a lack of understanding and sympathy throughout the whole appointment. The only person that understood and handled the situation with respect was Nick. Clearly the office was busy today, maybe over booked, maybe backed up and my son had to suffer because of it. I understand this but own it. Apologize for the wait, give a heads up, and don't cut corners on my sons appointment because you are running behind schedule. Take the time to do the work with the understanding that these young kids are scared and a little explanation and patience goes a long way. The TV and friendly wall paint doesn't matter as much as the rapor between patient and doctor/assistant. 

Our first filling went completely different and I can only put the pieces together to realize it was because the people working weren't backed up and in a rush. I don't care how many people are waiting. Do the job right all the time.

Switched my kid’s dentist to here recently, and I am so happy I did. The entire office is kid oriented, tv screens at each chair to keep kids occupied, kid painted scenery on the walls, and the staff is just amazing. We didn’t have a long wait, the assistants and dentist kept me and my 5 year old well informed. The office was clean and neat as well! They gift the kids with a balloon at the end (which is a huge hit)!

We switched over a few months back because of reviews and their hours. Dr. Maria has been great with each visit and office staff effective. My son underwent surgery last week and we were relatively pleased at the process. My son is doing great now :)
Love this office!!  All are very professional, get us in on time, and have great hours!
Dr Maria is wonderful. We are new patients and are very happy with this office. My kids don't mind going to the dentist at all!

TERRIBLE! Dr. Kritsineli answered her phone in the middle of my 5 year old daughter getting her cavities filled. She talked on the phone the entire procedure and touched my daughters mouth with her dirty fingers. Very unprofessional and unclean. She was no attentive to my daughter or her being nervous getting laughing gas because she was only concerned with her phone call. Very disappointed. Would never go back and never recommend.

Dr. Maria Kritsineli answered her phone in the middle of my 5 year old daughters procedure. She stayed on the phone the whole time, touching her phone and hair and then touching my daughters teeth and instruments. She did not change her gloves. It was disgusting and very unprofessional. My daughter had laughing gas and was getting two cavities filled and she was nervous. The dentist did nothing to make her feel better, because she was so busy on her phone call.  Horrible experience.
We just swtiched over to this office and it is wonderful!  Great place for kids!!  Wish they also treated adults!
Dr. Maria is awesome!

My son chipped his front permanent tooth pretty bad this morning (Sunday) I called the emergency number and his tooth was fixed that day! Every time I've taken my kids to the facility everyone is very curtious and prompt. Would highly recommend!

My son had to have dental surgery, put under general anesthesia in Boston. DCP handled all the paperwork, all the calls. All i needed to do was wait for the hospital to call and go down. DCP made the surgery easy, less anxieties overall. thank you so much from Jason and our whole family 👪
allReviews.response: Thank you very much for your trust Amber!! Doctor Maria and our office works hard for our litlle patients to give them the best dental experience!! 🦷🦷🦷

allReviews.comment: I have been bringing my 3 year old to Dental Center Pediatrics for about a year. I’ve always had a good experience. They are great with his wiggles and his giggles. But after this week I am recommending to everyone I know because of the experience I had with Dr. Maria Kritsineli in an emergency situation. It was outstanding and I am so thankful for her. This past week I had an unfortunate event happen to my 10 month old. The end result being a chipped tooth and a panicked call to the office at 8:45pm. I was expecting to leave a message and get a call in the morning, but I actually was able to call Dr. Kritsineli’s cell and send her a photo of the tooth. She explained thoroughly what to do and answered all of my questions during our phone call as well as the visit with her. I really can’t thank her and her team enough for making this experience a little less stressful than it already was!
Dr. Maria really yook care of my sons teeth issues. I recommend this office!

My son is 5, and I have been bringing him to see Dr. Maria for almost 2 years now! He recently had a filling, and had the best experience! He is a very energetic and curious little boy and Dr.Maria and her team did an amazing job putting him at ease in the chair, and explaining everything they were going to do! He also loves the video games in the waiting room! I highly recommend bringing your little ones here!!
Dental hygienist, Deanne, was AMAZING with my anxious kiddo. My son was a little nervous about his appointment today but that faded fast thanks to her! Deanne - you’re the best :)

I can not put into words how above and beyond Dr. Maria and her staff go for my kids. I could not imagine telling them that we were changing dentists! We had others in the past elsewhere and though they were pediatric dentist offices they do NOT rise to the level of care that you will get here! From the reception at the desk, the waiting room , the beautiful murals on every wall in every room, to the true comfort and kindness of the whole staff making your child and you feel at ease is amazing!! Just go there and take a look around and you will understand why I recommend them to every parent I know!
The staff are so sweet and are great with children! My daughter is very shy and they know how to make her feel comfortable and enjoys going there!

We’ve been informed that MA Health hasn’t remitted payment for our son’s visit and that unless we paid directly we could no longer schedule. MA Health has confirmed with myself as well as your office that he’s covered but you’re having issues collecting. This is an issue for your team to address directly with MA Health not for you to then turn to your patients and put the responsibility back on them. I’m horrified at such poor levels of customer support and services. We’ll be changing offices since I was told they simply don’t have time to deal with it. We’ll actively discourage anyone from using this office.
Great atmosphere and friendly staff. I've been taking my son here since he was 2 and 3 years later, still the same friendly and caring service.

Ok- Wow- this place is amazing! Honestly, I'm very hard to please. I knew when I called for the appointment it was going to be great! Just the whole atmosphere was inviting & warm. You would never think it was a dentist office. The front desk staff, to the dental assistants right to the dentist herself! I wish all my boys came here. I may just have to switch.
My son who is 4 1/2 & not the biggest fan of the dentist, was happy & without fear! That's a great place!
Highly impressed! Very friendly staff, great with the kids. Recommend to everyone.

We have a wonderful experience every time we visit our favorite dentist(7 years). Dr. Kritsineli is very knowledgeable and sweet. My 3 girls absolutely love her. I can’t thank her enough for being so patient and nice with my kids.

We were visiting family when my 16 month old fell and had an dental emergency right before bedtime.  I called Dr. Kritsineli and she kindly got us in to be seen the next morning.  She was so tender with our baby and explained extensively what the next steps should be.  If I did not live so far away, I would be going to this practice for everything!  Do not look further for your kids, choose Dr. Kritsineli and the amazing staff.

I have no idea how this place got so many great reviews. The dentist was forcing impression trays into my 6 yr old Granddaughters mouth and had to be asked if there were any smaller trays. She then took out trays that didn't hurt her to use. When we decided to take her elsewhere, the front desk person told me she didn't have time to email the Xrays,(she had 3 days notice) therefore delaying treatment of a badly abcessed tooth. I had to drive to their office to pick the xrays up only to find they gave me paper copies with the roots cut out of the pictures so the new dentist would be unable to see the infection. They also had another patients chart up when treating her. I also know someone else that they mixed up the correct patient as well. I would not recommend this place at all.
Great office, kind staff, and easy to schedule!

Great experience for kids! My son is 3 and my daughter is 18 months and they love going here! Only problem is Everytime I need to schedule an appointment both receptionists say they only schedule til 2 because they close mon-fri at 3...but when I look online that is untrue they do stay open later. Scheduling is the only difficult part.

I have a question for you guys. 
So, my son has the mass health plan. And he needs the pallet expender before we can talk braces. I was wondering if I could add him into my work dental plan as a secondary insurance, would that cover any of that expense?
Please advise
Jucelia Rosseti Gleason
allReviews.response:
Hello Ms Rosseti Gleason,
A private insurance most likely can cover interceptive orthodontic treatment (which is the palatal expander). There are some exceptions though. Before you enroll your son, please find out if the plan covers orthodontics. If yes, please find out if there is a medical necessary exception. 
In any case, if you give us the plan information we might be able to find out for you. 
Please let us know if we can assist you any further. 
Amie deals with the private orthodontic insurances in our office  and you can reach her @ 508-830-1212.    
Thank you,
Nicholas Gamouras

allReviews.comment: Sorry that was an error! You guys are great. No complaints!! My kids fo with no issues.
Always a great experience, they're so good with my toddler.

During my last appointment I had found out I had a cavity and now I was at the office to get it filled. The main dentist, Dr.Maria, put novicane around the area of my tooth, and began to drill. After about a minute of her drilling I knew something was wrong, then the drill hit the middle of my tooth, the most sensitive part. The Novocain DID NOT WORK and I was heavily crying in pain. I could feel every second of the drilling.When she saw I was in pain she asked if I was okay and I said NO, she proceeded to tell me that it was “almost done” and drilled my tooth for another 5 minutes while I was in agony. All I have to say NEVER GO TO DENTAL CENTER PEDIATRICS.
Everyone in the practice is so nice and caring.  They are always explaining what work they are doing and my kids are never scared.  We love Dental Center Pediatrics!

I take my daughter to this dentist and have since the first. She is 3 so I figured going to the dentist would be a struggle. But I have no complaints about anything. The staff is amazing and excellent with kids. The whole place is targeted specifically for kids with video games, and big comfy bean bag chairs in the waiting room, to toys and ballons as incentive after their appointment is finished. They also remind you of your appointment via text and communicate with you beforehand to remind you of upcoming appointments faithfully. They are very accommodating and work with you to make appointments based on your own schedule and availability.

I have been to Dental Center Pediatrics for my daughters dentistry. The issue I have is that they did not accommodate my request to have them notify me by personal  phone call about my daughters appointments. The service that they used would email me with a confirmation date a week before the appointment. Then a few hours before the appointment they would send a text. As an older single parent (father) whom is a self employed laborer and with limited computer skill I would forget the appointment. I talked to them about this issue each time this happened. Last appointment I brought this up in the office and the personnel assured me that this would not happen again. The office personnel and I agreed that they would call 1 day prior to the appointment and remind me when the appointment was so that I could make plans for the next day. On 12/27/18 I got a text at noon that said I had to meet them for an appointment at 4 pm. I was at work at this time and had to go through the frustration of this happening again. Completely caught me off-guard. I am no longer going to go through this again and will find another dentist.

Very kid friendly environment!! The Ladies in the reception area even helped keep an eye on my toddler while I filled out the new patient paperwork.  The hygienist and the dentist were both excellent with him and my little one was comfortable and relaxed for the whole visit!
Compassionate and caring. Very trustworthy and respectful. Always tells the patient and parent what’s going on. I recommend highly.
Friendly and knowledgeable team who are great with my daughter

I would not recommend you.  This was our third visit to your office.  I specifically told the receptionist that Henry had autism, although this should have been clearly written in his file.

I had to hold him down by his hips with my legs for 10 minutes in between the hygienist and the dentist.  This should have been a streamlined process.  My life with a special needs child is hard enough.  

I don't feel that your office did its best job to insure he had as pleasant an experience as possible.  Especially when that is what you are advertising.
Ur so friendly and my kids loved this place
It was my granddaughters first visit she had a great experience they also were very helpful while I filled out all the forms
My little guy's first dental appointment was a breeze. Friendly staff and overall good experience.

This office is a 20-minute drive for us, but I wouldn't do it if it wasn't worth it!  Dr. Maria is awesome!  She explains everything thoroughly to me AND my child.  Staff is friendly and helpful to wade through the dental insurance. Highly recommend this office!
I have a very satisfied experience every time my kids have an appointment at dental center pediatric
No complaints!! My son absolutely loves his trips to the dentist!! Everyone is beyond friendly and amazing with my kiddo. Thank you for making our visits “easy peasy lemon squeezy” as he would say :)

Dr. Kritsineli is the Best and our Favorite Dentist ever, and we’ve had quite a few before we finally found her seven years ago! She is always sweet and professional. All my three girls love her and always leave the office happy.

I was referred here when my little one needed oral surgery and I have to say they have been nothing but amazing. I just switched my children over from the previous dentist who referred us, although they were great as well. But they just know what they are doing with children at Dental Center Pediatrics!!!!
Awesome team of professionals . They are great with the children they provide care to. Highly recommend.
My daughter actually gets excited to go to the Dentist. It is a kids playland with friendly, knowledgeable and caring staff. 💗😃😍
Absolutely LOVE this office and how great they are with my daughter!
I honestly can't say enough positive things about Dr. Maria and her staff. My daughter absolutely LOVES going to the dentist because of them!!!.

My son has been a patient for close to five years now. After suffering an injury at daycare when he was two, we had our first visit. He loves going there, goes in by himself and has never been afraid of the dentist! All the staff are wonderful, receptionists, hygienists and doctors. I hope he never knows the anxiety of going to the dentist as many of us experience. One of the best choices I've made!
They make going to the dentist an easy experience on my kids (and myself!) Def recommend.

My son feels comfortable there.  He is usually very uncooperative when it comes to the dentist, but they have helped him come a long way for the exams. Im very grateful

Great experience- very organized, great dentist

I love dental center pediatrics! The dentist is alwaya so nice to my son and they have a lot for the kids to keep them entertained. I have never had to wait more than a couple minutes for an appointment and the appointment itself is quick which is great with a toddler. Highly reccomend them!
Great staff and doctors! Always accommodating and helpful. My girls actually get excited about going to the dentist.
Love love love this office.... Everyone here is so nice can’t say enough about them all. So I’m not going to what I am going to say is that I would recommend them to everyone and I have

allReviews.comment: Outstanding experience! Highly recommend!!

Wonderful!
Very friendly, caring and give you peace of mind. 
All staff are friendly and respectful.highly recommend

Amelia always likes to go to the dentist. The staff are the best...She has no fear or concerns when she gets there..I give this location in Plymouth five stars...Thank You all for making my grand daughters smile a beautiful one..
Wonderful atmosphere!  Everyone from the office staff to the hygienist and the dentist are knowledgeable and friendly and great with children.
From Emma:I had a great time I thought the dentist was really kind and nice 👍 I would love to go back there again!!!
We drive 2 hours each way to see Dr. Kritsineli for our daughter. Need we say anything else?
My kids love the staff and atmosphere of the Dental office. Even when they have to have work done they always are eager to go for the next visit.

My two boys had disappointments yesterday.  When their appointments were over no one walked out to tell me anything about the appointments.  I didn't see a dentist or have anyone tell me what they had done to them.   I waited around for a few minutes but then left.
Everyone was super nice and made my 3 year olds first visit to the dentist so easy!
Great pediatric dentistry team! Always impressed!

Was very worried about how my son was going to handle getting a cavity filled today due to past time wasn't so good on his party but he was amazing and Dr Maria and her staff was absolutely great with him.  I can't say enough Thankyou to Dr Maria and her staff
Dr Kritsineli and staff are wonderful, she makes sure going to the dentist is a good experience for children. The staff is warm and welcoming and the hours are fantastic.
Great staff. My 2.5 year and feels comfortable there and isn't afraid to get her teeth cleaned.
I was beyond thrilled that you were able to get Avas teeth fixed today. I was not sure how well she would do. You were so sweet, comfoffing and efficient. Thank you so much. Highly recomend you!

My son has been going to Dr. Maria and her wonderful staff for 4+ years. They are ALWAYS so happy, eager to help, and patient. I cannot emphasize "they are patient" enough. My son has sensory issues and with all the sounds, lights, flashes, etc he can break down a bit. They work so hard to remind him that they are willing to wait until he is ready....and I appreciate that.
My daughter was excited and happy to tell me all about her appointment today. I guess I am waiting for an email about scheduling another appointment.
We LOVE the staff at Dental Center Pediatrics!
Excellent group!

Very friendly and helpful staff and Dr. Maria was wonderful with my son - who can tend to be a nervous little guy. I found this place by recommendation from a friend and would most definitely recommend this place to others!
Dr. Maria, Dr. Karissa and all the staff are absolutely awesome. I take all my kids here and so do my friends. They treat your kids like their own and the work is excellent!
Always great!

My children have been going for over a year. They are wonderful they really put the kids at ease. You never have to wait more than a few minutes. The office is very clean and bright happy place. I would highly recommend Dental Center Pediatrics
My 3.5 year old has been going here since she was about 20 months old.  Both the hygentist and dentist are always so patient and gentle. Highly recommend

From the front desk to Dr. Maria I couldn’t ask for more. The front desk took care of everything including scheduling and insurance verification for 3 different insurances. It was such a project but she did it patiently and was so nice. Dr. Maria was amazing. She explained everything in great detail and found something in my 10 year old that no other dentist had picked up. So knowledgeable and very sweet with my daughter. Took a great deal of time with teaching and explanation. Highly recommend!
Always a great experience!

Dentist is a mom that gets toddlers
My toddler had to have a cavity filled, a first for her, and both of us were a little nervous (me more than her, to be honest)...
The staff was friendly and realistic, spoke to my daughter like they really cared. They have Tv's with kids movies over all the chairs, and have cutesy names for all the procedures. Overall, they made a tough situation, easy.

Alleviated fear and hard to numb tooth
Dr Kritsineli was wonderful! She was able to help alleviate my daughters fear of feeling pain in a tooth that just wouldn’t numb. She was able to numb it and fix it without issue. Very knowledgeable and help moms fears as well! Thank you!

High equality service and friendly staff
It's always to see the same staff and awesome service. It's always nice to see Dr. Maria.

Awesome bedside manners
I have a 5 yo autistic boy. We were here for his 6month check up. Everything was great until the woman brought out the spin brush for the fluoride. My son, started to have a bit of a fit and the woman knew what to do. She was absolutely amazing ! She assured my son she wasn't going to hurt him, she let him hold the tool and feel it spin. After all that, he calmed down and all was well. She took him to get a few prizes and a balloon. My son was in heaven. They're really great here. I would DEFINATELY recommend them.

My daughter was 2.5 and fell causing her front tooth to push out. We went to see Dr. Maria for a consult, she took an xray and said the root did not appear damaged,  but just in case she recommended pulling the tooth. I asked if that procedure would be done at the hospital, she said no, she would pull it in the office,  but due to my daughters age, she cannot receive novacaine unless in a hospital... so she wanted to pull my 2.5 year olds front tooth without novacaine. She also said to avoid using sippy cups.  We left immediately and I called Duxbury Children's dentistry for a 2nd opinion.  Dr Joy took new xrays and agreed there was no root damage. She told us to start using sippy cups again immediately as they would help push the tooth back into proper position.  She monitored the tooth every 6 weeks.  It did move back to proper position, healed,  and stayed in my daughters mouth until she was 5.5 and it fell out as normal baby teeth do and her adult tooth grew in fine.  I'm still shaken when I think of the pain and trauma Dr. Maria wanted to inflict on my toddler.  I would not recommend her to anyone.

We love Dr Maria! With three children (two having braces) we see her a lot!! Always so friendly and caring. It may sound funny but she’s like part of the family now. My children all smile when they see her. The whole staff is wonderful.

Highly professional and great with kids
Amazing. My daughter is 3 and had already been traumatized by a prior dentist office. Her experience today was great. They staff anf hygienist were all very nice and made my daughter feel safe. She walked out with a bright smile and i got to save my Aleve..lol. I recommend this office anyone with children.

Highly impressed!
I have been to many dentists over the years between myself and 5 children. I usually end up being a one time patient due to the lack of compassion, care or patience. 3 of my children recently had checkups and my 2 boys who both have dissabilities, 1 of which is on the Autism spectrum were treated with the absolute best care. Dr. Koster and her assistant were so kind, positive and patient and my 8 yr old ASD son has severe anxiety and you never would have known it. He was able to ask questions...a lot of them...and have them answered. I never felt like the Dr. was annoyed with my son or minimized his 8 yr old concerns. To have my son sit so calmly, not cry or have an episode while he had his sealants put in is a testament to this practice. Dental Center Pediatrics know how to work with children...all kinds of children and I am so happy to have found them. Even though they are a 45 min drive for us it is worth it to have a Dentist and staff that care about what they do!

So awesome!
The best

Great service, friendly staff
I have a 2 and 4 yo. We have been going to Dental Central Pediatrics for 1 year now. My children have met with both Dr Kritsineli and Dr Koster and both have been excellent with my young children. The hygienist are wonderful too. The front staff is friendly and efficient. The office and tools appear to be clean/sterile-which is very important!
I will continue to go and also recommend this pediatric dental center to others.
Kenny was as pleased as ever...thank you

My kids love it there! Doctors and staff are very patient and caring. They always go out out there way to make the child feel comfortable. Best pediatric dentist around! Definitely recommended!
Our doctor today was great!! It was our first visit (18 months) and it was made so easy by the staff.

Flouride
Everything went quickly and smoothly for all my children! We love going here!!!

Great visit as usual
Great visit today, thank you for being so good with the kids, you work hard to make them as comfortable as you can and I can say, it is very much appreciated by the parents.

Staff are extremely professional, efficient, and knowledgeable.
I absolutely am so impressed with Dental Center Pediatrics. We are new patients and I can honestly say that your group has gone above and beyond for us. The professionalism and the knowledge that you have met us with is superb.

Amazing!

The one recommendation I would make is especially for older kids like my son (almost 11) to give them a little more information before starting the process....talk them through what is going to happen in advance.....it felt like things moved very quickly at the beginning....there was a crying child in the next room and he was anxious....once the process started the doctor told Caiden what was going to happen next but I think a little of advance talk prior to nitrous, etc. would have been great.
Thank you again for taking such good care of him.

allReviews.comment: Recently had a horrendous experience with my 3 year old getting a sticky spot fixed. Little to no comfort/reassurance of my daughter (she screamed/cried the whole time) and little/no communication with my husband during the half hour procedure, during check out my husband over heard the staff say she had 3 more spots that needed to be addressed (there was no mention of this when they found the first spot 3 days prior), charged us on the spot without checking insurance coverage. Not going back

Our experiences with Dental Center Pediatrics have always been extremely positive. It's easy to get there, and easy to park. The office is beautiful, accommodating both children and adults very nicely. They've been professionally decorated, with a hand painted aquarium theme which flows throughout the office areas. There are big, fun beanbag chairs for the kids in the center of the reception room, as well as a television, video games, children's books and magazines. Adults are seated around the beanbags in elegant, comfortable chairs, with plenty of neatly arranged magazines. The reception staff is professional, kind, warm, and efficient. We've rarely had to wait more than five minutes to go in. Parents can be with and see their children through the entire visit. X-rays are done quickly, and a full orbital (?) machine is readily available so that you can bring whole mouth X-rays to orthodontists. Dental chairs and equipment are new and immaculate. Children watch a movie while the work is being done. The hygienists are professional and focused. They take the time to answer all questions and are helpful in motivating kids to brush and floss. Dr. Kritsinelu is perhaps the kindest, warmest professional I've ever met. She puts my child completely at ease. She is conservative, doing what is necessary, and only that. She takes the time to explain to parents and children what is going on, keeping everyone relaxed and informed. Kids leave with the usual goodie bag of a new toothbrush etc., as well as a prize. They have a great appointment reminder system, and do a great job with scheduling. Dr. "Maria" (Kritsinelu) has done a perfect job of providing excellent dental care in a comfortable environment with a terrific support team. I only wish she could treat adults!

reflected by the care she provides to her patients.  Its a team effort here and everyone here takes pride in their work. allReviews.comment:
Not the most open-minded people
I was looking for a holistic dentist, but could not find one for my daughter. So, I brought my daughter here and was completely upfront about my beliefs. When I was asked for more details, I provided them. The dentist swore up and down that she respected my beliefs and made her recommendations. She agreed to send me additional information regarding ingredients and safety of the dental products they use. The hygenist was very interested specifically in naturally curing cavities with nutrition, so we happily conversed during x-rays. I was contacted the very next day by the same dentist (who said she would honor my natural approach) and was told that "it's not a good fit" and they want their staff to be comfortable and that I should take my daughter somewhere else. I was nothing but kind to everyone there, my child is well mannered, so I'm not quite sure how I made anyone uncomfortable. Needless to say, if you are looking for a more natural approach for caring for your child's teeth, search elsewhere.
allReviews.response:
Dear Mrs. Joseph,
In response to your review online, we at Dental Center Pediatrics respect everyone’s beliefs and opinions. Please accept our apologies for not making it immediately clear we are not a holistic dental practice. We are not certified in this practice of dentistry, therefore we do not meet the criteria you are seeking. All of the materials used here are ADA approved and we follow their guidelines when it comes to treatment for all of our patients. If we accepted your daughter as a patient, it would be a disservice to you and your daughter because our materials are not classified as “all natural” or holistic.
Once again, I do apologize for the confusion. If you would still like to discuss this further, I will be more than happy to speak with you.
Sincerely,
Dr. Maria Kritsineli DMD

allReviews.comment:
Always wonderful and compassionate people
My son has been going to this office for a few years now. Everyone I have met has always been wonderful and compassionate, especially since my child is differently abled.
I was; however, (1) disappointed about the dental restorative care. It would be a better practice if they treated it like adults. No one called about the co-pays or discuss my child's ability to sit or what extra precautions could be used to minimalize the scare.
(2) I knew we would owe something, but was shocked to find out how much and what I would need to pay out of pocket because something was not covered by insurance. That should not happen. Those expectations should be laid out before arrival.
(3) The hygienist did not complete the xrays and the doctor did not know she needed to take xrays until after all the numbing medicines had been administrated.
Upon finding my child could not tolerate an office visit to handle his dental needs, the doctor was so compassionate and empathized with me. I was not expecting to need to organize a surgery into an already busy summer. But the office staff explained things right away. Gave me documents, and told me to call them with any and all questions.
Wish that was there approach for the office visit too. Perhaps some of the difficulty could have been avoided on both the doctor's, office, and my behalf.
GREAT
Everyone in the office was great. I also loved the murals on the walls.
allReviews.response: Thank you so much for your kind comments. We are glad that your trip to our office was a good one. We also love the murals. They are like a mini escape.

From the minute we walked in the door we were attended to by a staff that truly understands and obviously enjoys working with children. The hygenists worked quickly and calmly to get my son's x-rays done and keep him comfortable, and the doctor did the same. Dr. Maria needed to extract my son's tooth and she was always careful to keep all the tools out of the child's line of sight, and work very quickly to get the procedure done. The only time he was upset (not from pain or any procedure she was doing, rather just being scared of having to breathe through his nose- go figure) she calmly told him just tell me what makes you scared and I will fix it, no problem. And when he was still scared she stopped and said no problem, we won't make him do it- we have to do whatever he is comfortable with. I can say I wish this was the dental experience I had as a child, and that I could go see her myself! Thank you Dr. Maria & all the staff!!!!
